steps: Check: no further simplification is possible since $3x$ and $9$ are unlike terms.
That matches choice C — C is correct.

choices.elimination:
• Distribute: $3(x+5) = 3x + 15$, then subtract 6 to get $3x + 9$.
  • $3x - 3$ would result from $3 \times (x+1) - 6$ or a similar expression, not this one.
  • Eliminated because the constant is +9, not −3.

• This constant −1 does not arise from $15 - 6$.
  • A common error might be computing $5 - 6 = -1$ without distributing the 3 first, but the 3 must be distributed before subtracting 6.
  • Eliminated because $3(5) - 6 = 9$, not −1.

• Distribute: $3(x+5) - 6 = 3x + 15 - 6 = 3x + 9$.
  • This is the correct simplification. That matches choice C — C is correct.

• This would arise from multiplying both x and 5 by 3 incorrectly, treating the whole as $15x - 6$.
  • The distributive property gives $3x + 15$, not $15x$.
  • Eliminated because $3 \times x = 3x$, not $15x$.
